Brittany needs to calculate her total shampoo usage for a leap year, which has 366 days, by first determining she washes her hair on 183 occasions since she does so every other day. Multiplying the 183 washes by the 1/4 ounce used per wash results in a total consumption of 45.75 ounces, meaning she will need to purchase [number] bottles to cover this amount since each bottle contains 14 ounces.

To find the exact count, dividing the total 45.75 ounces by the 14 ounces per bottle yields approximately 3.27, indicating she must buy 4 bottles to ensure she does not run out before the year ends. She can store the extra bottles at her [location] until she needs them, ensuring she has enough product for the entire leap year without interruption.
